name: Janice Robinson
abstract: Janice Elaine Robinson (born December 28, 1951, Clairton, Pennsylvania) is an American jazz trombonist and trumpeter. Robinson played trombone on The Bill Cosby Show in her teens, at which time she also played trumpet, and received a bachelor's degree from the Eastman School of Music in 1973.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: New York Jazz Repertory Company
Universal Symphony presents an Evening of Chamber Music

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes
about_quotes.evidence: I had male and female vocalists, but lady instrumentalists — Janice Robinson the trombonist, and Sharon Freeman, a French horn and pianist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Schenectady, New York, March 28, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: When we had the World of Trombones, the principal trombone player was a woman. She was automatically the musical director... Janice Robinson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Byron Stripling
about_quotes.evidence: He’d just cut off the whole band and Janice Robinson would just play trombone with her plunger... I remember myself seeing that band a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Byron Stripling interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 25, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: We talked together the other day... She was thanking me for, you know, giving her that respect.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Arnett Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: Trombone was Janice Robinson, a gal playing trombone. You know Janice.
